intro: Randia schoemanii, commonly known as the Kudu apple or Wilde Appel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits in reducing inflammation and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Bioactive constituents of Randia schoemanii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been identified as responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into herbal teas and infusions, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or syrup, to harness its therapeutic potential.
